description_long: This is an elongated, irregularly shaped stone with a roughly oval profile and a slightly flattened top and bottom. Its surface is predominantly gray with subtle brown and greenish undertones, featuring natural textural variations and a few visible cracks and chips. The stone appears weathered, with some smoother areas and others that are more rugged, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements. One end is more rounded while the other tapers to a blunter, more angular point, giving it an asymmetrical silhouette. The stone's surface shows faint mottling and layering, with no artificial carvings or markings. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural obstacle or decorative element in urban parks, sidewalks, or landscaped areas.
description: An elongated, weathered gray stone with subtle mottling, cracks, and a mix of smooth and rough surfaces. Its irregular shape and natural texture make it a realistic urban obstacle or landscape feature.
description_view_0: The stone appears oval and slightly flattened, with visible surface texture and a prominent crack running along its side.
description_view_1: This end view highlights the stone's tapering, blunter point and shows a smoother, darker patch on the surface.
description_view_2: The opposite side reveals a lighter, more uniform surface with less pronounced cracks and a subtle greenish tint.
description_view_3: From this end, the stone looks rounded and shows a smoother, paler surface with faint layering.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and faint layering with natural cracks; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Moderately weathered with a mix of smooth and rough areas, some surface chips and cracks indicating exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, grass, or gravel, and may partially contrast with paved surfaces or nearby vegetation.
